● **India has not yet ratified the Agreement.** India is in the process of ratifying the Agreement. **So,** **statement IV is not correct.** **So, only two of the statements given above are correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies has officially entered into force after two-thirds of its member countries submitted their instruments of acceptance.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Agriculture (Schemes, APMC, MSP, Exports, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to India’s foreign trade, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 The total annual value of exports from India
has steadily increased between 2014-15 and 2024-25.
II. 	 In 2024–25, the share of the services sector
in India’s total exports is higher than that of merchandise exports.
III. 	The engineering goods sector constitutes the
largest component of India’s merchandise exports.
IV. 	 The Export Promotion Mission focuses on
tariff-impacted sectors and non-traditional districts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India’s exports have seen a historic rise, hitting an all-time high of US$ 825.25 billion in 2024–25, reflecting a robust 6.05% annual growth.
● The total annual export value **has not increased steadily** every year during the last decade i.e. between 2014-15 and 2024-25. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● **Merchandise exports** were **$437.4 billion** in 2024-25. **Services exports** were **$387 billion** in 2024-25. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● India’s merchandise exports in the fiscal year 2024-25 were significantly bolstered by agriculture, pharmaceuticals, electronics, and engineering goods, which collectively contributed over 50 percent of the total, govt data released on May 16 showed.
○ The **engineering goods sector** emerged as the most significant contributor, **accounting for 26.67** **percent** of India’s total exports, which amounted to USD 437.42 billion in 2024-25. Meanwhile, agriculture, pharmaceuticals, and electronics made up 11.85 percent, 6.96 percent, and 8.82 percent, respectively. **So, statement III is correct.** ○ Notably, the **electronic goods sector** achieved the **highest growth rate in exports,** soaring by
32.46 percent from USD 29.12 billion in 2023-24 to USD 38.58 billion in 2024-25. In the previous fiscal year, exports stood at USD 23.6 billion in 2022-23 and USD 15.7 billion in 2021-22.
● The **Export Promotion Mission (EPM)**, approved by the Government of India, has an outlay of **₹25,060** **crore for 2025–26 to 2030–31**. It aims to strengthen India’s export ecosystem by improving access to **affordable trade finance** and enhancing **global market readiness and competitiveness**.
○ The Mission focuses on **tariff-impacted sectors** and **non-traditional districts**, with priority to textiles, leather, gems and jewellery, engineering goods, and marine products, while remaining flexible to support emerging sectors. It targets **MSMEs, first-time exporters, and labour-** **intensive value chains** to ensure inclusive export growth. **So, statement IV is correct.**
○ EPM is implemented through two sub-schemes: **Niryat Protsahan**, which provides financial enablers by easing access to trade finance, and **Niryat Disha**, which offers non-financial support such as quality compliance, branding, trade fairs, logistics assistance, and capacity-building at the district and cluster levels.
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The Government has approved the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) with ₹25,060 crore to boost exports.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Essential Commodities Act,
1955, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It defines the term ‘essential commodities’.
II. 	 Both the Central and State Governments can
add or remove commodities from the list of essential commodities in public interest.
III. 	Fertilisers and petroleum products are not
covered under the Act.
Which of the statements given above are **not** correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **The Essential Commodities Act, 1955 (ECA)** was enacted to regulate or control the production, supply, distribution, trade, and commerce of certain commodities that are essential to the general public.
● **The Essential Commodities Act does not define the term ‘essential commodities’ in detail.** It authorises the Central Government to declare certain commodities as “essential” and regulate their production, supply, distribution, storage, and pricing. The objective, as clearly stated in the Act, is to secure equitable distribution and availability of these commodities at fair prices in the public interest. **So,** **statement I is not correct.**
● **Under the Act, the power to declare a commodity as essential or to remove it from the list lies with** **the Central Government**. However, once a commodity is notified as essential, State Governments are empowered to issue control orders for regulating supply and distribution within their jurisdictions. States do not have independent powers to add or remove commodities from the Schedule. **So, statement II is** **not correct.**
● **The Essential Commodities Act, 1955, covers items listed in its Schedule. These include drugs** (as defined under the Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940); **fertilisers** (organic, inorganic, and mixed); foodstuffs, explicitly including edible oilseeds and edible oils; hank yarn made wholly from cotton; **petroleum and** **petroleum products**; raw jute and jute textiles; and seeds, namely seeds of food crops, fruits and vegetables, cattle fodder, and jute. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the .**
**Relevance:** The Essential Commodities Act has been in the news due to amendments introduced in recent years to deregulate agricultural commodities and stock-limit relaxations for cereals, pulses, and oilseeds.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Manufacturing
questions.questionText:
Consider the following items :
I. 	 Research and Development (R&D) expenditure
II. 	 Mailing list of clients
III. 	Acquisition of specialized manufacturing
robots
IV. 	 Undeveloped land
How many of the above are considered Intangible Investments?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **A capital asset** is broadly defined as any property owned by a business or individual that is held for longterm use and is not intended for sale in the ordinary course of business; it includes **tangible (physical)** **and intangible (non-physical) assets** that provide future economic benefit.
● **R&D can represent intangible investment** because it builds future economic value through new products, processes, or technologies. Many modern business assessments consider R&D as part of intangible assets since it enhances innovation capacity and competitive advantage - even though it may not always be capitalized under accounting standards unless specific criteria are met. **So, point I is** **correct.**
● **A mailing list of clients is a classic intangible asset** because it has no physical form but has economic value - for example, in marketing, customer retention, and future sales. Such databases or customer lists are recognised as intangible resources that contribute to business success. **So, point II is correct.**
● This is a tangible asset — a physical piece of machinery used in production. It holds value and assists in generating future economic benefits, but it is not intangible because it has a physical form. **So, point III** **is not correct.**
● **Undeveloped land is a tangible capital asset — physical property that an enterprise holds. It is not** **an intangible resource, although** it may have future potential value; it is a physical asset. **So, point IV** **is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: India and the World (USA, Russia, Middle East, Africa, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the Yamuna River:
I. 	 It originates from the Yamunotri Glacier in
the Garhwal Himalayas.
II. 	 It flows through Uttarakhand, Haryana, Delhi,
and Uttar Pradesh only.
III. 	The catchment area of the river accounts for
around two-fifths of the Ganga River basin.
IV. 	 The Tons River, originating from the
Bandarpunch Glacier, is its largest tributary.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The Yamuna River is a perennial river of northern India and the **largest tributary of the Ganga**. It plays a critical role in irrigation, drinking water supply, hydroelectric power generation, and cultural life across several states.
● It rises from the Yamunotri Glacier in the Garhwal Himalayas in Uttarakhand, close to the Bandarpunch massif in the Himalayas. **So, statement I is correct.**
● After originating in the Garhwal Himalayas, it passes through **Uttarakhand**, briefly touches **Himachal Pradesh**, then flows along or through **Haryana** and the **National Capital** **Territory of Delhi**, before traversing **Uttar Pradesh** and finally joining the Ganga River at Prayagraj. **So, statement II** **is not correct.**
● The catchment area of the Yamuna River accounts for around **two-fifths of the Ganga River basin. So, statement III is correct.**
● The **Tons River** is the **largest and most significant tributary of the Yamuna**. It originates near the **Bandarpunch Glacier** in the Garhwal Himalayas and joins the Yamuna at Kalsi in Uttarakhand. The Tons contributes **nearly 60% of the Yamuna’s total water volume.  So, statement IV is correct.**
○ Other significant tributaries of Yamuna are Giri, Chambal, Hindon, Betwa, Ken, Sind, Dhasan and Sabi. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** A thick layer of toxic froth resurfaces on the Yamuna River in Delhi

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Physiography of India (Location, Division, Physiographic Regions)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Hill/Mountain
Range
Physiographic Division
I. - Darjeeling Himachal Himalayas
II. - Male
Mahadeshwara
Vindhya Range
III. - Kalvarayan Eastern Ghats
IV. - Rajgir Chota Nagpur Plateau
How many of the pairs given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India’s physiography is marked by a great diversity of hills and mountain systems formed through different geological processes.
● **The Darjeeling Hills are located in northern West Bengal and form part of the Lesser Himalayas**, also known as the **Himachal Himalaya**. They lie south of the Great Himalayan range and are characterised by steep slopes, high rainfall, and deep valleys. **So, pair I is correctly matched.**
● **Male Mahadeshwara Hills** (MM Hills) is a major pilgrimage center located in the dense forests of the **Eastern Ghats** in Karnataka. It is home to the revered Sri Male Mahadeshwara Temple, an ancient shrine dedicated to Lord Shiva (in the form of Mahadeshwara). **Together with the Biligiriranga Hills**, the hill range forms a **geological bridge** between the **Eastern and the Western Ghats. So, pair II is not** **correctly matched.**
● **The Kalvarayan Hills are located in Tamil Nadu and represent a segment of the Eastern Ghats**. The Eastern Ghats are not a continuous mountain range but a series of broken hills and plateaus, of which the Kalvarayan Hills are a prominent part. They consist mainly of ancient metamorphic rocks and rise between the Kaveri basin and the coastal plains. **So, pair III is correctly matched.**
● **The Rajgir Hills are located in Bihar and form part of the Rajgir–Giriyak hill system, which is an** **extension of the Chota Nagpur Plateau**. This plateau is a part of the Peninsular Plateau and is composed of very old crystalline rocks. The Rajgir Hills are residual hills formed due to long-term denudation processes typical of the Chota Nagpur region. **So, pair IV is correctly matched.** **So, only three of the given pairs are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Strict safety restrictions were imposed at Male Mahadeshwara following a fatal leopard attack on a pilgrim. Rajgir hills emerge as major winter haven for migratory birds

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
With regard to nuclear energy in India, consider
the following statements:
I. 	 Nuclear power contributes less than 5% to
India’s total electricity generation.
II. 	 India has set a target of achieving 30 GW of
nuclear power capacity by 2031–32.
III. 	Natural uranium is the primary fuel used in
India’s present nuclear power reactors.
IV. 	 The Tummalapalle uranium belt in Andhra
Pradesh is the largest known uranium reserve
in India.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● India’s nuclear energy programme plays a strategic role in ensuring energy security and low-carbon power generation. Managed by the **Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited** (NPCIL), it relies mainly on indigenous Pressurised Heavy Water Reactor (PHWR) technology and follows a three-stage nuclear programme to optimally use uranium and thorium resources. Nuclear power contributes a modest but important share to India’s electricity mix while supporting long-term sustainability goals.
● India’s total nuclear installed power capacity exceeds 8180 MW. **Nuclear energy contributes roughly** **3% of India’s electricity generation**, well below 15%. This low share reflects historical constraints related to fuel availability, high capital costs, and long gestation periods of nuclear projects. **So, statement** **I is correct.**
● The Government of India, through the Department of Atomic Energy, has officially stated **a target of** **achieving approximately 22.48 GW of installed nuclear power capacity by 2031–32**. The government has set an ambitious target of **100 GW of nuclear power capacity by 2047**. This expansion includes indigenous Pressurised Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs) and reactors under international cooperation. **So,** **statement II is not correct.**
● **Natural uranium is the primary fuel used in India’s present nuclear power reactors**. India’s operational nuclear reactors are predominantly Pressurised Heavy Water Reactors (PHWRs), which use natural uranium as fuel and heavy water as moderator. This design choice was made to optimize the use of limited domestic uranium resources and avoid dependence on enrichment technologies in the early phases of the nuclear programme. **So, statement III is correct.**
● **The Tummalapalle uranium deposit in the Kadapa basin of Andhra Pradesh is the largest known** **uranium reserve in India**. **So, statement IV is correct.**
○ **Jharkhand** is an old mining region with Uranium mines in the Singhbhum Shear Zone and the existence of other key uranium areas in **Meghalaya, Rajasthan**, and **Karnataka** is largely accurate. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: India and the World (USA, Russia, Middle East, Africa, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following information:
Mineral Highest Reserves in the World
Highest Rese India
I. - Vanadium China Arunachal Pr
II. - Graphite Madagascar Jharkhand
III. - Nickel Indonesia Odisha
IV. - Tungsten Australia Andhra Prad
In how many of the above rows is the information

options.d:
All the four
ves in Primary use
desh High-strength steel and aerospace alloys
Refractory materials and lithium-ion batteries
Stainless steel production
h Cutting tools and electrical applications
orrectly matched?

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Vanadium** is a critical alloying metal used to **improve the strength, hardness,** and **corrosion resistance** **of steel**, especially for high-speed tools, jet engines, and aerospace components. Vanadium in India is now primarily associated with **Arunachal Pradesh**, following significant discoveries by the Geological Survey of India in the Siang belt. These findings have established Arunachal Pradesh as the country’s **largest holder of primary vanadium resources**. At the global level, **China** possesses the highest vanadium reserves and is also the leading producer**. So, row I is correctly matched.**
○ Within India, **Karnataka** has historically been known for vanadium occurrences, but these are largely **secondary resources**, recovered as a by-product from magnetite ores rather than primary deposits. Vanadium is a critical mineral mainly used in the production of high-strength steel and advanced alloys, including titanium alloys.
● **Graphite** is a non-metallic mineral with **high thermal stability and electrical conductivity.** Its primary uses include **refractory linings, lubricants,** and, increasingly, as an **anode material** in lithium-ion batteries. Graphite shows a more nuanced distribution pattern. **China** holds the world’s largest natural graphite reserves, followed by **Brazil**. While **Madagascar** is a major producer, but not the largest reserve holder. In India, **Arunachal Pradesh** holds the **largest share of graphite reserves**, accounting for about **43%** of the total, followed by **Jammu and Kashmir** with around **37%**. Smaller but notable shares are found in **Jharkhand** (6%), **Tamil Nadu** (5%), and **Odisha** (3%). **So, row II is not correctly matched.**
○ Graphite production, however, is concentrated mainly in **Tamil Nadu (37%)**, **Jharkhand (30%)**, and **Odisha (29%)**, highlighting a clear difference between **where reserves are located** and **where active mining and production occur** in the country.
● **Nickel** is an essential metal for producing **stainless steel and corrosion-resistant alloys.**  Nickel resources in India are highly concentrated in **Odisha**. These deposits are mainly associated with the Sukinda ultramafic belt. At the global level, **Indonesia** holds the largest nickel reserves. Nickel is strategically important, with nearly two-thirds of global consumption devoted to the manufacture of stainless steel, making it a key industrial and strategic mineral. **So, row III is correctly matched.**
● **Tungsten** is valued for its exceptionally high melting point and hardness. It is primarily used in **hard** **metals, cutting and drilling tools,** and certain **electrical and electronic applications**. Tungsten presents a contrast between resource distribution and historical mining. Globally, **China** dominates tungsten reserves and production. In India, **Karnataka** holds the **largest share of total tungsten resources**. However, **Rajasthan**, particularly the Degana area, has traditionally been the **only significant tungsten-** **producing region** in the country. Tungsten’s exceptionally high melting point makes it indispensable for hard metals, cutting tools, and electrical applications. **So, row IV is not correctly matched.** **So, in only two of the rows is the information correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** India recently prioritised vanadium, graphite, nickel, and tungsten under the National Critical Minerals Mission to reduce import dependence and support clean energy.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Physiography of India (Location, Division, Physiographic Regions)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements correctly
distinguish between the Tropical Cyclones and the
Extra-Tropical Cyclones?
I. 	 Tropical cyclones possess a distinct frontal
system, whereas extra-tropical cyclones are
driven by thermal convection.
II. 	 While the extra-tropical cyclones originate
over both land and sea, the tropical cyclone
can originate only over the sea.
III. 	Tropical cyclones generally move from east
to west, while extra-tropical cyclones move
from west to east.
IV. 	 Extra-tropical cyclones are usually more
destructive and affect a much smaller area
than tropical cyclones.
Select the correct answer using the code given
below:
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Cyclones** are a type of atmospheric disturbance where wind circulates around a low-pressure centre. Based on their location, cyclones are classified as tropical cyclones and extratropical cyclones.
○ **A tropical cyclone** is a low-pressure system that develops over warm tropical waters. It is a rapidly rotating storm, where high-speed air circulation occurs around the low-pressure centre. They are known as **Cyclones in the Indian Ocean**, **Hurricanes in the Atlantic, Typhoons in the Western** **Pacific and South China Sea**, and **Willy-willies in Western Australia.** ○ **Extra- tropical cyclones** occur in temperate and high-latitude regions. These systems derive their energy from the release of potential energy when cold and warm air masses interact.
● The extra-tropical cyclone differs from the tropical cyclone in a number of ways:
○ **The extra-tropical tropical cyclones have a clear frontal system,** which is not present in the tropical cyclones. (Front is a weather system that is the boundary between two different types of air masses). **So, statement I is not correct.** ○ The **extra-tropical cyclones can originate over land and sea,** whereas the **tropical cyclones** **originate only over the seas,** and on reaching land, they dissipate. **So, statement II is correct.** ○ The **extra-tropical cyclones usually move from west to east,** but tropical cyclones move from east to west. **So, statement III is correct.** ○ The **extra-tropical tropical cyclones cover a larger area** compared to tropical cyclones. The extra-tropical cyclone affects a much larger area as compared to the tropical cyclone. The wind velocity in a tropical cyclone is much higher and it is **more destructive than the extra-tropical** **cyclone**. **So, statement IV is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Cyclone Ditwah caused large-scale damage in Sri Lanka/Cyclonic storm Montha touched the coast of Andhra Pradesh

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with regard to
the atmospheric humidity:
I. 	 The Relative Humidity of air does not change
with temperature as long as the total amount
of moisture remains constant.
II. 	 The Dew Point is the temperature that a parcel
of air would reach if all the water vapor in the
air parcel were condensed.
III. 	The latent heat is released into the air when
the water vapour is condensed into water
droplets.
IV. 	 The wet-bulb temperature is the lowest
air temperature that can be achieved by
evaporating water from the air.
How many of the statements given above are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Water vapour** present in the air is known as **humidity.** It is expressed quantitatively in different ways.
● **Absolute Humidity:** The **actual amount** of water vapour present in the atmosphere is known as the absolute humidity. It is the weight of water vapour per unit volume of air and is expressed in terms of grams per cubic metre.
● **Relative Humidity: The percentage of moisture present in the atmosphere as compared to its full** **capacity to hold water at a given temperature** is known as the relative humidity.
○ The ability of the air to hold water vapour depends entirely on its temperature. With the change of air temperature, the capacity to retain moisture increases or decreases and the relative humidity is also affected. ○ A warmer air parcel can hold a greater amount of water. **So, if the temperature of an air parcel** **is increased, provided the amount of moisture remains constant, its relative humidity would** **decrease**. As air temperature increases, air can hold more water molecules, and its relative humidity decreases. When temperatures drop, relative humidity increases. **So, statement I is not** **correct.**
● The **Dew Point** is the temperature to which air must be cooled (at constant pressure and moisture content) to reach saturation (100% relative humidity). At this point, water vapor begins to condense; it **does not** **mean all vapor has condensed**. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● When the water vapour is condensed into water droplets, heat is released into the air. The heat released is known as the **latent heat of condensation**. **So, statement III is correct.**
○ Condensation is the process of conversion of water vapor into liquid water. Condensation is caused by the loss of heat.
● **Wet-bulb Temperature** (Twb) is the **lowest temperature to which air can be cooled** by the evaporation of water into the air at a constant pressure. **So, statement IV is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** A new study found out that between 2012 and 2022, the relative humidity has significantly increased in North India, particularly in Indo-Gangetic Plain.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
Which of the following forces is/are currently
recognised by geologists as the primary cause for the movement of tectonic plates?
I. 	 Mantle convection
II. 	 Ridge push
III. 	Tidal force
IV. 	 Polar-fleeing force
V. 	 Slab pull
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

options.d: I, II, III and V only

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● A **tectonic plate** (also called a lithospheric plate) is a massive, irregularly-shaped slab of solid rock, generally composed of both continental and oceanic lithosphere. Plates move horizontally over the asthenosphere as rigid units. These plates have been constantly moving throughout the history of the Earth.
● **Convection currents** are present in the mantle portion of Earth. They are formed by the movement of fluid in the mantle portion. They are getting generated in the asthenosphere by the excessive heat available there. Heat transfer from the interior of the Earth to the surface results in the formation of huge convection current cells in the mantle. **The slow movement of convection currents in the mantle portion is** **considered by many as one of the main driving forces behind the plate movement. So, option I is** **correct.**
● In addition to convection currents, two other forces may play significant roles in plate movement: **slab** **pull and ridge push.**
○ **Slab pull** occurs when a dense oceanic plate sinks into the mantle at a subduction zone, pulling the rest of the plate along with it. **Ridge push** happens at mid-ocean ridges. This push is caused by gravitational force, and it exists because the ridge occurs at a higher elevation than the rest of the ocean floor. As rocks near the ridge cool, they become denser, and gravity pulls them away from the ridge. As a result, new magma is allowed to well upward from the underlying hot mantle. **So, options II and V are correct.**
● Alfred Wegener suggested that the movement responsible for the drifting of the continents was caused by **polar-fleeing force** and tidal force. The polar-fleeing force relates to the rotation of the earth. However, most scholars considered this force to have a lesser impact. **So, option IV is not correct.**
● Although the **Tidal force** is considered to affect the subduction of plates, it is not generally considered a primary driver of plate motion. **So, option III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Recent studies suggest that Africa is undergoing a dramatic geological transformation that could eventually divide the continent into two separate landmasses.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water on Earth Surface, Ocean Relief
questions.questionText:
Consider the following information:
Landform Geomorphic Agent
Geomorphic Process
I. - Esker Glacier Deposition
II. - Yardang Wind Deposition
III. - Loess Oceanic Waves Erosion
IV. - Uvala Ground Water Erosion In how many of the above rows is the information correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Erosion** is the geological process in which Earth materials are worn away and transported by natural forces such as wind, glaciers, waves or water. **Deposition** is the laying down of sediment carried by wind, flowing water, the sea or glaciers.
● Geomorphic agents like **running water, groundwater, wind, glaciers, and waves perform** erosion and deposition. Because of these, changes occur on the surface of the Earth. Each geomorphic agent produces its own assemblage of landforms.
● As the geomorphic agents are capable of erosion and deposition, two sets of landforms are produced by them: erosional/destructional and depositional/constructional.
● **Eskers** are ridges made of sand and gravel, **deposited by glacial meltwater.** These are long, narrow, sinuous ridges composed of sand and gravel which mark the former sites of sub-glacial melt-water streams. They vary from a few feet to 200 feet in height and may be several miles long. As eskers are made up of highly porous sand and gravel, water is rapidly drained off from their crests and they may not support many trees. **So, row I is correctly matched.**
● **Yardangs** are formed by the **erosional activity of** **winds**. Yardang consists of parallel **troughs separated** **by ridges**. Yardangs involve ridges separated from one another by long grooves. It is a ‘ridge and furrow’ landscape. They are **formed when wind erosion** **cutsthe  desert floors** of relatively softer rocks. They are formed where the hard and soft rocks are aligned in the direction of the prevailing winds. **Wind abrasion** **excavates the bands of softer rocks** into long, narrow corridors, separating the steep-sided overhanging ridges of hard rocks. They are mostly found in dry desert regions. **So,** **row II is not correctly matched.**
● **Loess is the landform formed by the depositional activities** **of winds** in deserts. Loess is a large accumulation of windblown sediments that covers a significant portion of the land surface. They are formed when wind blows fine dust from the desert and deposits it beyond the desert limits on neighbouring lands. Thus, loess builds up at the edges of deserts. For example, as wind blows across the Gobi of Asia, it picks up and carries fine particles. **So, row III is not** **correctly matched.**
● **Uvalas** are a type of landform found in karst/limestone terrains. Uvalas are a particular type of depression **formed by groundwater erosion of soluble limestone.** When sinkholes and dolines join together because of slumping of materials along their margins or due to roof collapse of caves, long, narrow to wide trenches called valley sinks or Uvalas form. **So, row IV is correctly matched.**
○ A sinkhole is a depression, more or less circular at the top and funnel-shaped towards the bottom. Dolines are large openings or a void in a karst region. **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Volcanic dust reflects microwaves effectively,
making it easily detectable on standard weather radar.
II. 	 LiDAR systems are ineffective at detecting
volcanic ash due to the presence of glass-rich silicate particles in it.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● When a volcano erupts, it ejects fragmented material into the atmosphere known as **tephra.** The **coarsest** **tephra**, particles larger than 64 mm, are classified as **blocks and bombs** and typically fall close to the volcanic vent. **Intermediate-sized fragments,** such as **lapilli (2–64 mm) and volcanic ash (≤2 mm)**, are carried upward by the heat of the eruption and are deposited at progressively greater distances from the volcano. The finest particles, termed **volcanic dust** and measuring **less than 0.01 mm**, can remain suspended in the atmosphere for two to three years, enabling long-range transport and climatic influence.
● Standard weather radars primarily use **microwave wavelengths** to detect hydrometeors such as rain, snow and hail. **Volcanic ash and fine volcanic dust** particles are often too **small and irregular** to effectively reflect microwaves. As a result, volcanic ash clouds are **not reliably detectable on conventional weather** **radar,** except when ash particles are aggregated with moisture. **So, statement I is not correct.**
● **LiDAR** **(Light Detection and Ranging)** is a **remote-sensing technology** that uses laser beams to measure precise distances and movement in an environment, in real time. LiDAR data can be used to generate everything from detailed topographic maps to the precise, dynamic 3D models that are required to safely guide an autonomous vehicle through a rapidly and constantly changing environment.
● **LiDAR** systems are **highly effective** in detecting volcanic ash. They use laser light, which interacts strongly with the **glass-rich silicate particles** present in volcanic ash. This makes LIDAR a valuable tool for monitoring ash concentration, altitude and dispersion. **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (d) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The Hayli Gubbi volcano, located in Ethiopia’s Afar region about 800km northeast of Addis Ababa near the Eritrean border, erupted on Sunday (November 23, 2025) for several hours.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
System Primary Technology Used
I. - Integrated Drone Detection & Interdiction System (IDD&IS)
Micro-rockets and micro-missiles
II. - Bhargavastra Electronic jamming and Laser-based neutralisation
III. Indrajaal AI-enabled detection, jamming and spoofing
IV. - D4 Anti-Drone System
Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS)
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Integrated Drone Detection & Interdiction System (IDD&IS)** is an indigenously developed counter-drone platform by the **Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)** and is designed to detect, track and neutralise hostile unmanned aerial vehicles. It integrates multiple sensors like **radar, electro-optical/infrared (EO/IR) and radio-frequency (RF)**, with both soft-kill measures such as **electronic jamming** and **hard-kill options including laser-based neutralisation.** The Mark-2 variant, equipped with an enhanced 10 kW laser, extends engagement ranges up to 2 km, enabling rapid and precise protection of critical installations against emerging drone threats. **So, pair I is not correctly** **matched.**
● **‘Bhargavastra’** is a low-cost, indigenous **Counter-Drone System** developed by **Solar Defence and** **Aerospace Limited (SDAL)** to counter the growing threat of **hostile drone swarms.** It offers a unified solution for detecting and neutralising small unmanned aerial vehicles at ranges of up to **2.5 km.** Bhargavastra can be deployed across diverse terrains, including high-altitude regions exceeding 5,000 metres above sea level, thereby meeting the specialised requirements of India’s armed forces.
● The system follows a layered interception architecture: ○ The first layer employs **unguided micro-rockets,** capable of neutralising multiple drones simultaneously within a lethal radius of about 20 metres. ○ The second layer consists of **guided micro-missiles,** enabling high-precision engagement of specific targets. **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
● In a major enhancement to **India’s border defence architecture**, **Indrajaal Drone Defence** has launched **‘Indrajaal Ranger’**, the country’s **first Anti-Drone Patrol Vehicle (ADPV)**. This AI-powered defensive grid uses a combination of **jammers, spoofers and real-time intelligence to secure up to 4,000 sq km.** **So, pair III is correctly matched.**
● The **Drone Detect, Deter and Destroy System (D4S)** is India’s **first indigenously developed** anti-drone system to be inducted into the Indian Armed Forces. It was developed by the **Defence Research and** **Development Organisation (DRDO)** and manufactured by **Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL),** the system is designed to counter rogue drones posing threats to vulnerable and high-value installations.
○ D4S incorporates both **soft-kill and hard-kill capabilities:** it can detect and electronically jam micro-drones while also employing a laser-based neutralisation mechanism for physical destruction. The system utilises **DRDO-developed RF and Global Navigation Satellite System** **(GNSS) technologies** to identify the control frequencies of hostile drones and disrupt them through targeted jamming, enabling rapid and effective counter-UAS operations. **So, pair IV is correctly** **matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Drone and related technologies in the defence sector have been very much in limelight after Operation Sindoor.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: Governor Generals and Their Policies, Administrative Developments, Judicial Developments, Civil Services, Police, Military
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Biologics are complex, large-molecule drugs
produced using living organisms or their
biological components.
II. 	 Biosimilars are identical drugs derived
from non-biological sources and match the
reference biologic exactly in structure, safety,
and efficacy.
Which of the statements given above is/are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Biologics and biosimilars form a critical component of modern therapeutics, especially in the treatment of chronic and complex diseases such as autoimmune disorders, cancer, and diabetes. Unlike conventional small-molecule drugs, these medicines are produced using living systems, which introduces structural complexity and variability.
● **Biologics** are medicinal products **derived from living organisms or their biological systems** and include substances such as monoclonal antibodies, vaccines, hormones, and recombinant proteins. Due to their biological origin, biologics are typically large, complex molecules with intricate structures, and their production involves advanced biotechnological processes.  **So, statement I is correct.**
● **Biosimilars** are biological medicines developed to be **highly similar, but not identical, to an already** **approved reference biologic.** Because biologics are complex and produced in living systems, exact replication is not possible, and therefore biosimilars may have minor differences in structure while demonstrating no clinically meaningful differences in safety, purity, or efficacy. **They are not derived** **from non-biological sources.** **So, statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct .**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Biologics: Large-molecule drugs produced using living systems • Examples: Insulin, monoclonal antibodies, vaccines • Biosimilars: Highly similar versions of approved biologic drugs • Key distinction from generics: Cannot be identical due to biological variability
**Relevance:** Biocon Biologics to introduce three new biosimilars for cancer treatment.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Information and Communication Technology, Robotics, AI
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:** Microwaves can penetrate through clouds, dust and rain to reveal the Earth’s surface.
**Statement II:** Microwaves have shorter wavelengths and higher energy compared to visible and infrared radiation.
Which of the following is correct with respect to the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Microwaves** form an important part of the electromagnetic spectrum and are extensively used in remote sensing, satellite communication, and weather monitoring due to their ability to interact uniquely with atmospheric components.
● **Microwaves** occupy the longer-wavelength region of the electromagnetic spectrum, typically ranging from about 1 mm to 1 m. **Their relatively long wavelengths allow them to penetrate clouds, dust,** **smoke, and light to moderate precipitation with minimal scattering.** This property makes microwavebased remote sensing, such as **Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR)**, especially valuable for Earth observation, as it enables continuous monitoring of the Earth’s surface regardless of weather conditions or daylight availability. **So, statement I is correct.**
● In the electromagnetic spectrum, **energy decreases with increasing wavelength.** Since microwaves have longer wavelengths than visible and infrared radiation, they possess lower energy, not higher. Visible and infrared radiation have shorter wavelengths and higher energies compared to microwaves. **So, statement** **II is not correct.** **So, Statement I is correct but Statement II is not correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** NISAR and related technologies were in news after the successful launch of the satellite by the USA and India.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: IPR, Institutions and Policies for IPR
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Conduct electricity only along its edges.
II. 	 Efficiently convert heat into electricity at the
nanoscale.
III. 	Be a safer alternative to lead in quantum dots.
How many of the above are properties and applications of bismuth metal in its 2D state?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● In its two-dimensional form, bismuth can behave as a **topological insulator**, meaning electric current flows mainly along its edges while the interior remains insulating. This edge-only conduction reduces energy loss due to resistance, making 2D bismuth promising for **energy-efficient electronic devices**. **So,** **statement I is correct.**
● Two-dimensional bismuth exhibits strong thermoelectric properties at the nanoscale, allowing it to efficiently convert heat energy into electrical energy. This makes it useful for **nanoscale thermoelectric** **devices**, such as waste-heat recovery and micro-power generation. **So, statement II is correct.**
● Bismuth is relatively **less toxic than heavy metals like lead**. In its 2D form, it is therefore being explored as a **safer alternative in quantum dots**, especially for optoelectronic and biomedical applications where toxicity is a concern. **So, statement III is correct.**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Bismuth (Bi): Heavy metal with atomic number 83 and low toxicity • 2D bismuth (bismuthene): Atomically thin sheets enabling quantum confinement • Topological insulators: Materials conducting electricity only along edges or surfaces • Quantum dots: Nanoscale semiconductors where electrons occupy discrete energy levels due to quantum confinement • Advantage of bismuth: Strong diamagnetism, electronic stability, and safer alternative to lead • Applications: Quantum devices, spintronics, low-energy computing, sensors
**Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The development of 2D bismuth links advances in condensed matter physics with next-generation electronics, quantum computing, and sustainable materials.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which of the following hormones are primarily
responsible for promoting growth in plants?
I. 	 Auxins
II. 	 Gibberellins
III. 	Cytokinins
IV. 	 Ethylene
V. 	 Abscisic Acid
Select the correct answer using the code given below:

options.b: I, II, III and IV only

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Plant hormones (phytohormones)** are natural chemical substances produced in plants that regulate growth, development, and responses to environmental stimuli. They can **promote or inhibit growth**, influence cell division, elongation, differentiation, and coordinate various physiological processes.
● **Auxins:** Auxins are primarily responsible for **cell elongation, apical dominance, root initiation, and** **vascular differentiation**. They directly promote plant growth by stimulating the elongation of stem cells and organizing tissue development. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Gibberellins:** Gibberellins stimulate **stem elongation, leaf expansion, and seed germination**. They break seed dormancy and drive rapid growth, making them essential for vertical growth and reproductive development. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Cytokinins:** Cytokinins primarily **promote cell division (cytokinesis) and differentiation**, influencing organ development and delaying leaf senescence. They work together with auxins to regulate overall plant growth. **So, point III is correct.**
● **Ethylene:** Ethylene influences certain developmental processes like **fruit ripening and cell expansion**, but since it does **not directly promote cell division or elongation and can sometimes inhibit growth**, it is not classified as a primary growth-promoting hormone. **So, point IV is not correct.**
● **Abscisic Acid (ABA):** ABA mainly acts as a **growth inhibitor**. It regulates seed dormancy, stomatal closure, and stress responses, helping the plant survive adverse conditions rather than directly promoting growth. **So, point V is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct .**
**Relevance:**

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Climate Change and Impacts of Climate Change over Earth
questions.questionText:
With reference to India s Environmental Accounting on Forests, 2025”, consider the following statements:
I. 	 It shows an increase in provisioning and
regulating services over the last decade.
II. 	 It follows the UN System of Environmental-
Economic Accounting (SEEA) framework.
III. 	It was released by the Ministry of Environment,
Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
**Environmental-economic accounting** integrates ecological data with economic statistics to assess the contribution of natural resources to national development. **Forest accounting** captures changes in forest assets, ecosystem services, and their economic value. Environmental Accounting on Forest – 2025 marks a major institutional step in this direction.
● The Forest Services Account (2025) records an increase in provisioning services (timber and non-timber products) as well as a significant rise in regulating services, especially carbon retention, during 2011 2022.. **So, statement I is correct.**
● **India adopted the UN System of Environmental-Economic Accounting (SEEA) framework in 2018**, and the forest accounting publication has been compiled using this internationally accepted framework. **So, statement II is correct.**
● The **report was released by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI)**, not by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC). **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Environmental Accounting: Integration of environmental data with economic accounts • SEEA Framework: UN-adopted system for environmental-economic accounting • Provisioning services: Timber and non-timber forest products • Regulating services: Carbon sequestration and climate regulation
**Relevance:** In 2025, the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) compiled and released the 8th consecutive issue of the publication related to environment accounts titled “Environmental Accounting on forest -2025”.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Soil Pollution, Solid Waste, Land Degradation
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pollutants:
I. 	 Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs)
II. 	 Dioxins
III. 	Mercury
IV. 	 Furans
V. 	 Sulphur dioxide
How many of the above are direct byproducts of plastic production or degradation?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
Plastics and plastic waste, especially when burned or improperly incinerated, release a range of highly toxic pollutants into the environment. Many of these chemicals belong to the category of **Persistent Organic** **Pollutants (POPs),** which persist for long periods **and bioaccumulate.** Understanding their sources is crucial for pollution control and public health.
● **Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs)** are released during incomplete combustion of plastics and mixed waste in landfills and open dumping, as highlighted in landfill fire incidents. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Dioxins** are unintentional byproducts formed during plastic manufacture, waste incineration, and burning of chlorinated plastics, and are classified as POPs. **So, point II is correct.**
● **Mercury** is a toxic heavy metal mainly released from coal combustion, mining, and industrial processes, **not a direct byproduct of plastic manufacture or degradation.** **So, point III is not correct.**
● **Furans** are formed alongside dioxins during the burning and thermal degradation of plastics and other chlorinated materials. **So, option IV is correct.**
● **Sulphur dioxide** is primarily released from fossil fuel combustion and smelting of sulphur-containing ores, not directly from plastics. **So, point  V is not correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Persistent Organic Pollutants (POPs): Toxic, long-lasting chemicals that bioaccumulate • Plastic burning releases: PAHs, dioxins, furans, heavy metals, fine particulates • Dioxins and furans: Unintentional industrial byproducts • Major sources: Landfill fires, waste incineration, open dumping of plastics
**Relevance:** Recent reports on landfill fires and plastic pollution show that burning plastic waste releases toxic POPs, severely harming human health, ecosystems, and air quality, highlighting the need for stronger plastic waste regulation.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Distribution of Continents and Oceans
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
I. 	 Polar bears are found only in the Arctic Circle.
II. 	 No species of penguins are found north of the
Equator.
III. 	Some coral species are found in the Arctic
Circle.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The **global distribution of species** is governed by evolutionary **adaptation, climatic conditions, ocean** **currents, and habitat availability.** While polar and tropical regions are often viewed as biologically distinct, scientific discoveries have revealed notable exceptions. Understanding these patterns is important for biodiversity conservation and climate-related studies.
● **Polar bears (Ursus maritimus) are exclusively native to the Arctic region** and are distributed across areas surrounding the Arctic Ocean, including parts of Canada, Greenland, Russia, Alaska (USA), and Norway. They **are absent from the Antarctic and the Southern Hemisphere**, as their evolution and survival are closely tied to Arctic sea ice ecosystems used for hunting seals. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Although penguins are predominantly found in the Southern Hemisphere, **the Galápagos penguin** **(Spheniscus mendiculus) inhabits the Galápagos Islands located at the Equator.** Due to the influence of cold ocean currents such as the Humboldt Current, this species can survive in equatorial conditions, and some individuals are found slightly north of the Equator. **So, statement II is not correct.**
● Contrary to common belief that corals exist only in warm tropical waters, **several cold-water coral** **species are found within the Arctic Circle.** Reef-building species such as Lophelia pertusa and Flabellum macandrewi thrive in cold, deep waters with temperatures ranging from 4°C to 12°C, forming complex reef ecosystems that support high marine biodiversity. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Polar bears: Arctic-exclusive marine mammals dependent on sea ice • Penguins: Southern Hemisphere birds with a rare equatorial exception • Cold-water corals: Azooxanthellate corals adapted to deep, cold environments • Arctic coral reefs: Found on continental shelves and fjords
**Relevance:**

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Places in News and Geopolitical Hotspots (Ukraine, Taiwan Strait, Hormuz, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following reproduces asexually
through vegetative propagation using tubers, producing genetically identical offspring (clones), and at the same time, it can reproduce sexually through flowers and seeds, leading to genetic variation in offspring?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Potato (Solanum tuberosum)** is an important food crop with distinct anatomical and physiological adaptations and exhibits **both asexual and sexual modes of reproduction**. It reproduces **asexually** **through vegetative propagation using tubers**, where the “eyes” act as buds producing **genetically** **identical clones**, and also **sexually through flowers and seeds**, which introduces **genetic variation** essential for breeding and crop improvement. The potato tuber is a **modified underground stem**, as indicated by the presence of nodes (eyes), internodes, and scale leaves, and primarily functions in **starch** **storage**, enabling the plant to survive unfavourable conditions and regrow when conditions become suitable.
○ Potato plants also synthesise **toxic glycoalkaloids**, mainly **α-solanine and α-chaconine**, which are distributed throughout the plant and are especially concentrated in the **leaves, stems, flowers,** **fruits, sprouts, and the skin and eyes of tubers**. These compounds serve as a **natural defence** **against pests and herbivores**, but they are also significant from a **food safety perspective**, as excessive accumulation in edible parts can be harmful to humans.
● **Onion** reproduces vegetatively through **bulbs**, not tubers. Although it can produce flowers and seeds, it does **not use tubers** for asexual reproduction.
● **Turmeric** reproduces vegetatively through **rhizomes**, not tubers. Sexual reproduction is rare and the vegetative organ involved is not a tuber.
● **Ginger** also reproduces through **rhizomes**, not tubers, even though it can flower. Hence, it does not meet the condition of tuber-based vegetative propagation. **Therefore, option (a) is the correct .**
**Knowledge Box:**
• Potato tuber: Modified stem with nodes and internodes • Mode of reproduction: Both sexual (seeds) and asexual (tubers) • Glycoalkaloids: Solanine and chaconine – toxic defence compounds • Highest toxin concentration: Sprouts, flowers, fruits, and tuber skin
**Relevance:**Bio-fortified potatoes, with added iron content, will soon be available in Indian markets, said Director General of the Peru-based International Potato Center (CIP).

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: National Park, Wildlife Sanctuary, Biosphere Reserve
questions.questionText:
Consider the following:
I. 	 Pin Valley National Park
II. 	 Chandratal wetland
III. 	Sarchu Wildlife Sanctuary
IV. 	 Pangong Tso Lake
V. 	 Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary
How many of the above constitute the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The **Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve was designated by UNESCO in 2025 under the Man and the Biosphere** **Programme** to protect India’s fragile Trans-Himalayan cold desert ecosystems. It integrates protected areas, alpine lakes, wildlife habitats, and human settlements. Accurate knowledge of its spatial extent is important for conservation and exam-related questions.
● **Pin Valley National Park** forms a core ecological area of the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve. Located in the Spiti Valley of Himachal Pradesh, it represents typical cold desert landscapes, including alpine meadows, glacial valleys, and high-altitude plateaus, and supports species such as the snow leopard and Himalayan ibex. **So, point I is correct.**
● **Chandratal, a high-altitude alpine lake, and the Sarchu & Kibber Wildlife Sanctuary are explicitly** **mentioned as components of the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve.** These areas contribute to wetland ecosystems, migratory bird habitats, and cold desert biodiversity, strengthening the ecological representativeness of the reserve. **So, point II, III and V are correct.**
● Although **Pangong Tso Lake** shares Trans-Himalayan cold desert characteristics, it is geographically and administratively located in Ladakh, not in Himachal Pradesh. The Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve has clearly defined boundaries confined to the Lahaul–Spiti region. There is no officially recognised hydrological, ecological, or zonal linkage between Pangong Tso and the reserve. **So, point  IV is** **incorrect.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** UNESCO officially announced the designation of the Cold Desert Biosphere Reserve in India – as part of 26 new sites added to UNESCO’s World Network of Biosphere Reserves (WNBR) during the 5th World Congress of Biosphere Reserves in Hangzhou, China.

questions.subject: International Relations
questions.microTopic: Global Groupings and Agreements (FTA, Paris Agreement, NPT, QUAD, etc.)
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Kyoto Protocol, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It is the only global climate treaty that imposed
legally binding greenhouse gas emission reduction targets exclusively on developed
countries.
II. 	 The Clean Development Mechanism enabled developed countries to meet part of their emission reduction targets by funding mitigation projects in developing countries.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The **Kyoto Protocol** was an international treaty **under the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate** **Change (UNFCCC)** that **committed industrialized countries to reduce greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions,** **adopting legally binding targets for the first time, based on the principle of "Common But Differentiated** **Responsibilities".** Adopted in Kyoto, Japan, in 1997, it entered into force in 2005, establishing targets for developed nations to **cut emissions (like CO2, methane, nitrous oxide) below 1990 levels.**
● The Kyoto Protocol (1997, in force since 2005) remains the **only global climate treaty that imposed** **legally binding emission reduction targets** exclusively on developed (Annex I) countries. Developing countries were exempted from binding targets in line with the principle of Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities (CBDR-RC). Neither the Paris Agreement nor any subsequent treaty has imposed such binding targets only on developed countries. **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● The **Clean Development Mechanism (CDM)** was a flexibility mechanism under the Kyoto Protocol that allowed developed countries **to earn Certified Emission Reductions (CERs)** by financing emissionreduction or removal projects in developing countries. This helped developed countries meet their binding targets cost-effectively while promoting sustainable development and technology transfer in developing nations. **So statement II is correct.** **Therefore, option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** After the Paris Agreement came into effect in 2016, it was understood that the 1997 Kyoto Protocol had lost its legal relevance. However, the ICJ has now said that the treaty remains valid, and countries have to comply with its provisions.

questions.subject: Environment
questions.microTopic: Climate Change and Impacts of Climate Change over Earth
questions.questionText:
With reference to the Greenhouse Gas Emission
Intensity Target Rules, 2025, consider the
following statements:
I. 	 It sets 2023-24 as the baseline to reduce
emission intensity.
II. 	 It allows eligible industries to trade excess
reductions as carbon credits.
III. 	It authorizes the Bureau of Energy Efficiency
to impose penalties for non-compliance.
Which of the statements given above are
correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
The **Greenhouse Gas Emission Intensity Target Rules, 2025** set **legally binding emission reduction targets** for selected high‑emission industries, measured as GHG emissions per unit of output. **2023–24 is used as the** **baseline year**, and the rules aim to align industrial performance with India’s climate goals while enabling participation in the emerging carbon market.
● The rules use **FY 2023–24 as the baseline** to determine emission intensity targets for compliance periods. This provides a measurable reference for tracking reductions and ensures improvements are relative to actual historical emissions. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Industries exceeding their targets can receive **certified carbon credits**, which are **tradable** under India’s carbon market. This incentivizes over‑performance, while underperforming units can purchase credits to meet obligations. **So, statement II is correct.**
● While the rules impose penalties for non‑compliance, these are **not directly enforced by the Bureau of** **Energy Efficiency (BEE)**. BEE determines credit issuance and average market price, but enforcement and recovery are handled by environmental authorities like the Central Pollution Control Board) CPCB. **So, statement III is not correct.** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The Government of India notified the final greenhouse gas (GHG) emission intensity targets for the first industrial sectors to be covered under the compliance mechanism of the Carbon Credit Trading Scheme (CCTS)

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Interior of Earth and Continental Theories
questions.questionText:
Consider the following information:
Ramsar Site
State Distinct Physical Trait
I. - Keoladeo National Park
Rajasthan Man-made wetland
II. - Gokul Reservoir
Gujarat Coastal wetland
III. Kopra
Jalashay
Bihar Oxbow lake
IV. Lonar
Lake
Maharashtra Crater formed in basaltic rock of the Deccan Traps
In how many of the above rows is the information
correctly matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Keoladeo National Park**, located in the State of **Rajasthan**, is an important wintering ground of Palaearctic migratory waterfowl and is renowned for its large congregation of non-migratory resident breeding birds. It is a **man-made and actively managed wetland of international importance**, designated as a **Ramsar site** and serves as a critical habitat for **migratory waterfowl along the Central** **Asian Flyway**. It is the only national park in India entirely enclosed by a boundary wall approximately two metres high, leaving **no scope for the creation of a buffer zone** around the protected area. **So, row** **I is correctly matched.**
● **Gokul Reservoir** in **Bihar** is an **oxbow lake** located on the southern edge of the **Ganga (Ganges) River.** The flood pulses of the Ganges influence land use and land cover in the wetland, exposing marshes and agricultural areas during the dry months and increasing inundation after monsoons. It was declared as a **Ramsar Site in September 2025. So, row II is not correctly matched.**
● **Kopra Jalashay** is situated in **Chhattisgarh**, India. It is the first Ramsar Site in Chhattisgarh. It is a **man-** **made reservoir** located in the upper catchments of the **River Mahanadi.** It was originally constructed for irrigation purposes and it is mainly surrounded by farmland and a few villages. The wetland’s extensive open water area features shallow nutrient-rich backwaters. **So, row III is not correctly matched.**
● **Lonar Lake**, located in **Maharashtra**, is a unique **Ramsar wetland** formed due to a **hyper-velocity** **meteorite impact** around **50,000 years ago**. Unlike most impact craters worldwide, Lonar is exceptional because it was created in **basaltic rock of the Deccan Traps**, making it one of the **very few such craters** **globally**. **So, row IV is correctly matched.** **So, only in two rows given above is the information correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** India has 96 designated Ramsar sites (wetlands of international importance), making it one of the leading nations in Asia for wetland conservation.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.microTopic: Water in the Atmosphere (Clouds, Condensation, Precipitation, Rainfall, etc.)
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements:
**Statement I:**
Enhanced Rock Weathering (ERW) can be used as
a method to capture atmospheric carbon dioxide.
**Statement II:**
Elements like calcium and magnesium released
upon weathering of rock react with carbonic acid
to produce limestone.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of
the above statements?

options.a:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
and Statement II explains Statement I
options.b:
Both Statement I and Statement II are correct
but Statement II does **not** explain Statement I
options.c:
Statement I is correct but Statement II is
incorrect
options.d:
Statement I is incorrect but Statement II is
correct

questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Enhanced rock weathering** **(ERW)** is a process that accelerates natural chemical reactions in
which **rocks react with CO₂ in the air,** **converting it into stable carbonate minerals.** By doing so, atmospheric carbon is effectively removed and stored in solid form, making enhanced rock weathering a potential carbon dioxide removal technique to help mitigate climate change. **So, statement I is correct.**
● Elements like **calcium and magnesium released** **during the weathering of rocks** **react with** **carbonic acid or bicarbonate to produce** **limestone.** Carbonic acid (H₂CO₃) is simply **carbon dioxide dissolved in rainwater**, which makes the water slightly acidic, while bicarbonate (HCO₃⁻) is the ion **formed when carbonic acid reacts** **with water or dissolves in rivers and oceans.** The carbon in carbonic acid and bicarbonate then combines with calcium or magnesium to form stable carbonate minerals such as limestone. These chemical reactions form the key mechanism through which enhanced rock weathering captures CO₂ from the atmosphere. **So, statement II is correct.** **So, both Statement I and Statement II are correct and Statement II explains Statement I** **Therefore, option (a) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Enhanced rock weathering (ERW) and aims to speed up the natural capture and storage of carbon dioxide -- a planet-warming greenhouse gas. Crushed rock is being sprinkled across large stretches of farmland globally in a novel bid to combat climate change.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.microTopic: Biology and Biotechnology
questions.questionText:
Consider the following pairs:
Biomaterial category
Description
I. - Drop-in biomaterials
Chemically identical to petroleum-derived materials
II. - Drop-out biomaterials
Chemically similar to conventional plastics
III. Novel
biomaterials
Possess self-healing materials or bioactive implants
How many of the pairs given above are correctly
matched?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Biomaterials** are materials derived **wholly or partially from biological sources,** or produced using biological processes, that are designed to replace, supplement, or interact with conventional materials. Their use has expanded rapidly across various sectors, including packaging, textiles, construction and healthcare, driven by a focus on sustainability and technological innovation.
● Common examples include **bioplastics produced from plant-based sugars or starch,** bio-based fibres used in **textiles** and **medical biomaterials** such as biodegradable sutures and tissue-engineering scaffolds.
● Based on their chemical structure and compatibility with existing systems, biomaterials are broadly classified into three categories:
○ **Drop-in biomaterials,** which are chemically identical to **petroleum-derived materials** and can be seamlessly integrated into existing manufacturing and recycling infrastructure (e.g., bio-PET). **So, pair I is correctly matched.** ○ **Drop-out biomaterials,** which are **chemically distinct from conventional materials** and therefore require new processing technologies or specialised end-of-life management systems (e.g., polylactic acid – PLA). **So, pair II is not correctly matched.**
○ **Novel biomaterials,** which exhibit entirely new or enhanced properties not found in traditional materials, such as **self-healing capabilities, bioactivity** and advanced composite structures, enabling innovative applications in healthcare and high-performance materials. **So, pair III is** **correctly matched.** **So, only two of the pairs given above are correctly matched.** **Therefore, option (b) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** Indigenous biomaterials can reduce dependence on fossil-based imports.

questions.subject: Economy
questions.microTopic: Insurance and Pension
questions.questionText:
With reference to the inscriptional evidence of the
Gupta period consider the following statements:
I. 	 They present us evidence that the Guptas
and the Vakatakas of the Deccan were contemporaries.
II. 	 No woman was allowed to possess independent access to resources such as land.
III. 	Agrahara lands were not to be entered by
soldiers and policemen and were exempted from major and minor taxes.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● **Gupta period (c. 320–550 CE) inscriptions, such as the Allahabad Pillar Inscription** and Vakataka charters, provide clear evidence that the **Guptas and the Vakatakas were contemporaneous dynasties**, and they also record matrimonial and political connections between the two ruling houses. **So, statement** **I is correct.**
○ The **Poona copper plate** epigraphs inform that **Prabhavatigupta,** the daughter of **Chandragupta** **II (Vikramaditya)**, was married to crown prince **Rudrasena II**, the son of Prithvishena I of the Vakatakas dynasty.
● **According** to **contemporary Sanskrit legal texts** (Dharmashastras), women were generally not supposed to have independent access to resources like land. However, inscriptional records show that **women could** **and did possess land and other resources**. Several Gupta and post-Gupta inscriptions mention land grants made to women or owned by them, indicating independent property rights, at least among elite sections of society. **So, statement II is not correct.**
○ As per the Poona **copper plate** epigraphs Prabhavati Gupta did have access to land, as she granted the village of Danguna to a Brahmana.
● Numerous copper-plate inscriptions of the Gupta period record **Agrahara land grants to Brahmanas**,
which were often **exempted from major and minor taxes and placed beyond the jurisdiction of** **soldiers, policemen, and other state officials.** Such grants reflect the administrative structure and the growing practice of fiscal and administrative immunities. **So, statement III is correct.** **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** A team of researchers cited that climate change influenced the ascent and decline of major Indian dynasties: the Gupta empire, Gurjara-Pratihara and Chola empire.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: Literature of Ancient India
questions.questionText:
Which of the following Sangam era texts
provides a detailed genealogy and record of the achievements of the Chera kings?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● Sangam literature refers to a corpus of early Tamil literary works which were passed down as oral tradition. According to Nakkiranar who wrote a commentary on Iraiyanar Akaporul, a medieval work of 8th century CE, there were three Sangams. All the Sangams or academies of poets were patronised by the Pandya rulers. The present works are extant from the third Sangam, which include **Tolkappiyam** (ancient Tamil Grammatical work) **Ettu tokai** (Eight anthologies) and **Pattupattu** (ten long poems).
● **Purananuru** is a part of the eight anthologies. It deals with "external" (Puram) themes like war and kingship. However, it covers kings from all three dynasties—Cheras, Cholas, and Pandyas—rather than being a dedicated genealogical record for the Cheras alone. **So, option (a) is not correct.**
● **Tolkappiyam** is the most ancient surviving Tamil grammar text and the oldest surviving long work of Tamil literature, written by an ancient Tamil scholar named Tolkappiyar. The surviving manuscripts of the Tolkappiyam consist of three books (atikaram), each with nine chapters (iyal), with a cumulative total of 1,612 sutras. It includes sutras on orthography, phonology, etymology, morphology, semantics, prosody, sentence structure, and the significance of context in language. **So, option (b) is not correct.**
● The **Sangam** **poems** sporadically referred to the genealogy of kings. The three major kings, namely, the Cheras, Cholas the Pandyas are usually mentioned with their ancestry.
o One of the eight anthologies, **Patitrupathu, details the Chera history** with epilogues added in the later period, probably in the early medieval period. It gives a poetic description of the rule of the Chera kings. **So, option (c) is correct.**
● **Maduraikanchi** was **written by Mangudi Maruthanar**. This poem specifically describes the city of Madurai and the achievements of the Pandya king, Nedunchezhiyan. **So, option (d) is not correct.** **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** New research has shown that weavers during the Sangam period used natural, plant-based dyes such as indigo to colour their clothes.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.microTopic: Salient Features of the Indian Constitution & Comparison with Other Countries
questions.questionText:
With regard to classical languages in India, which
of the following statements are **not** correct?
I. 	 Classical language status is accorded by
the Ministry of Culture based on prescribed criteria reflecting cultural significance.
II. 	 One of the eligibility conditions, for classical
status, requires a high antiquity of early literature or recorded history spanning about 1,500–2,000 years.
III. 	Pali and Prakrit were the first two languages
to receive Classical status.
IV. 	 All languages recognised as Classical are
included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Ministry of Culture grants "Classical Language" status** to languages that meet certain specific criteria and provides support for the preservation and promotion of these languages. Designating a language as "Classical" serves as a formal tribute to its role as a cornerstone of India’s civilization. It essentially recognizes Cultural Legacy, Intellectual Wealth and Historical Continuity of Indian civilization. **So, statement I is correct.**
● The criteria for designating a language as a Classical Language were set by the Linguistics Experts Committee under the Sahitya Akademi. **The criteria are as follows:**
o **High antiquity** of **early texts/recorded history** over a period of **1500- 2000 years.  So, statement** **II is correct.** o A body of **ancient literature/texts,** which is considered a **heritage** by generations of speakers. o **Knowledge texts,** especially **prose texts** in addition to poetry, epigraphical and inscriptional evidence. o Classical Languages and literature could be distinct from their current form or could be discontinuous with later forms of their offshoots.Classical language status is an administrative category created by the Government (Ministry of Culture) to recognize languages with ancient roots and high antiquity.
● The **Eighth Schedule** of the Constitution of India currently has 22 languages. All languages designated as Classical Languages are not included in the Eighth Schedule of the Constitution of India. Pali and Prakrit (which are classical) are not in the Eighth Schedule. **So, statement III is not correct.**
● The Government of India decided to create a new category of languages as "Classical Languages" **on 12th** **October 2004, declaring Tamil as the first Classical Language**. **So, statement IV is not correct.**
● o Six languages - Tamil, Sanskrit, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, and Odia—were recognised as Classical Languages between 2004 and 2014. On October 3, 2024, the Union Cabinet approved adding **Marathi**, **Pali**, **Prakrit**, **Assamese**, and **Bengali** to this category, expanding the recognised Classical Languages to eleven. **Therefore**, **option (c) is the correct .**
**Relevance:** The Education minister of India has launched 41 literary works and 13 books on classical languages in a significant national initiative to preserve India's linguistic heritage.

questions.subject: History
questions.microTopic: Buddhism, Jainism & Other Philosophies
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements with reference
to the Ellora caves:
I. 	 It includes the ‘Teen Tal’, a three-storeyed
cave structure associated with a major Buddhist monastic complex.
II. 	 The Kailasa Temple is the largest monolithic
rock-cut temple in the world.
III. 	Most of the Brahmanical and Jain caves at
Ellora are attributed to the Rashtrakuta rulers.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanationMarkdown:
● The **Ellora caves, locally known as ‘Verul Leni’ has 34 monasteries and temples carved out of the** **basaltic hills in the Charanandri hills.** It showcases a spirit of co-existence and religious tolerance through the outstanding architectural activities carried out by the followers of three prominent religions: Buddhism, Brahmanism, and Jainism. The rock-cut activity was carried out in three phases from the 6th century to the 12th century.
o The **earliest caves (Caves 1–** **12)**, excavated between the 5th and 8th centuries, reflect the then-prevalent **Mahayana** **philosophy of Buddhism in** **the region**. The Brahmanical caves (Caves 13–29), including the renowned **Kailasa Temple** **(Cave 16)**, were excavated between the 7th and 10th centuries. The final phase, between the 9th and 12th centuries, witnessed the excavation of **Caves 30–34, reflecting Jaina philosophy.** o Cave 10 (Visvakarma or Sutar-ki-jhopari, the Carpenter’s cave), Cave 11, and **Cave 12 Teen Tal,** **or three-storied monastery** predominantly belong to the Buddhist tradition. **So, statement I is** **correct.**
● The **Kailasa Temple** (Cave 16) of Ellora caves is the **largest monolithic structure in the world**, carved out of a single piece of rock. The Kailasha Temple at Ellora was built by the **Rashtrakuta king Krishna** **I in the 8th century.** Unlike traditional buildings, it was excavated using a "top-down" technique where
sculptors started at the peak of the mountain and worked their way down to the base. **So, statement II is** **correct.**
